assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism limit of ~20 nodes simultaneously' and mentions it increases to 100 in roadmap

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: States 'upgrade requires ~150 batches minimum, taking days to weeks — far exceeding your 8-hour Saturday window' and provides detailed batch calculations

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: Recommends 'Extend maintenance windows to multi-day approach' and 'Stagger node pool upgrades by priority' with specific weekend scheduling examples

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends 'Week 1 — CPU pools (lower risk)' followed by 'Week 2 — GPU pools' and explains CPU pools have 'stateless workloads, easier to reschedule'

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: States 'GPU pools during training gaps' and provides specific guidance to 'Coordinate with training schedule' using maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: Shows concrete example of extending to 16 hours across Saturday+Sunday and recommends multi-weekend upgrade cycles

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: Does not mention cluster autoscaler limitations or how it interacts with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: Does not mention the 15,000 node standard limit or the 65,000 node limit for AI workloads in GKE 1.31+
